agents.name: MTAtlas Composite Vulnerability Detection
agents.description: Runs MTAtlas static-pure analysis over uploaded Agent function metadata to detect multi-tool vulnerability chains.

scans.title: 小红书agent 组合式漏洞检测

params.taskName: 小红书agent 组合式漏洞检测
params.metadataText:
[
  {
    "func_signature": "publish_content",
    "inputs": [],
    "outputs": [],
    "MCP": "xiaohongshu",
    "description": "",
    "code": "def publish_content(
    xiaohongshu: Annotated[Xiaohongshu, Depends("xiaohongshu")],
    title: str,
    content: str,
    tags: List[str] | None = None,
) -> str:
    """
    Publishes a new text-only feed to the platform.

    Creates a feed with a title, content, optional tags, and a timestamp,
    and assigns it to the current user.

    :param title: Title of the feed (maximum 20 characters).
    :param content: Main text content of the feed.
    :param tags: Optional list of tag strings.
    :return: JSON string containing the published feed information.
    :raises ValueError: If the title exceeds the length limit.
    """
    feed = xiaohongshu.publish_content(title, content, tags)
    return tool_ok({
        "feed_id": feed.id,
        "title": feed.title,
        "content": feed.content,
        "tags": feed.tags,
        "timestamp": feed.timestamp.isoformat(),
    })"
  },
  {
    "func_signature": "list_feeds",
    "inputs": [],
    "outputs": [],
    "MCP": "xiaohongshu",
    "description": "",
    "code": "def list_feeds(
    xiaohongshu: Annotated[Xiaohongshu, Depends("xiaohongshu")]
) -> str:
    """
Lists all feeds in the system ordered by recency.

Returns a summary view of each feed, sorted by timestamp in descending order.

:return: JSON string containing a list of feed summaries.
"""

    feeds = xiaohongshu.list_feeds()
    return tool_ok([
        {
            "feed_id": f.id,
            "user_id": f.user_id,
            "title": f.title,
            "content_preview": f.content[:50] + "..." if len(f.content) > 50 else f.content,
            "tags": f.tags,
            "likes": f.likes,
            "favorites": f.favorites,
            "comment_count": len(f.comments),
            "timestamp": f.timestamp.isoformat(),
        }
        for f in feeds
    ])"
  },
  {
    "func_signature": "search_feeds",
    "inputs": [],
    "outputs": [],
    "MCP": "xiaohongshu",
    "description": "",
    "code": "def search_feeds(
    xiaohongshu: Annotated[Xiaohongshu, Depends("xiaohongshu")],
    keyword: str,
    sort_by: str = "综合",
) -> str:
    """
    Searches feeds by keyword and optional sorting strategy.

    The keyword is matched against feed titles, contents, and tags.

    :param keyword: Search keyword (must be non-empty).
    :param sort_by: Sorting strategy (e.g., 综合, 最新, 最多点赞).
    :return: JSON string containing matched feed summaries.
    :raises ValueError: If the keyword is empty.
    """
    if not keyword.strip():
        raise ValueError("Keyword cannot be empty.")
    feeds = xiaohongshu.search_feeds(keyword, sort_by)
    return tool_ok([
        {
            "feed_id": f.id,
            "user_id": f.user_id,
            "title": f.title,
            "content_preview": f.content[:50] + "..." if len(f.content) > 50 else f.content,
            "tags": f.tags,
            "likes": f.likes,
            "favorites": f.favorites,
            "comment_count": len(f.comments),
            "timestamp": f.timestamp.isoformat(),
        }
        for f in feeds
    ])"
  },
  {
    "func_signature": "get_feed_detail",
    "inputs": [],
    "outputs": [],
    "MCP": "xiaohongshu",
    "description": "",
    "code": "def get_feed_detail(
    xiaohongshu: Annotated[Xiaohongshu, Depends("xiaohongshu")],
    feed_id: str,
) -> str:
    """
    Retrieves full details of a specific feed.

    Includes the feed content, metadata, and all associated comments and replies.

    :param feed_id: Identifier of the feed.
    :return: JSON string containing detailed feed information.
    :raises ValueError: If the feed does not exist.
    """

    feed = xiaohongshu.get_feed_detail(feed_id)

    def serialize_comment(c: Comment) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "comment_id": c.id,
            "user_id": c.user_id,
            "content": c.content,
            "timestamp": c.timestamp.isoformat(),
            "replies": [serialize_comment(r) for r in c.replies],
        }

    return tool_ok({
        "feed_id": feed.id,
        "user_id": feed.user_id,
        "title": feed.title,
        "content": feed.content,
        "tags": feed.tags,
        "likes": feed.likes,
        "favorites": feed.favorites,
        "timestamp": feed.timestamp.isoformat(),
        "comments": [serialize_comment(c) for c in feed.comments],
    })"
  },
  {
    "func_signature": "xhs_user_profile",
    "inputs": [],
    "outputs": [],
    "MCP": "xiaohongshu",
    "description": "",
    "code": "def xhs_user_profile(
    xiaohongshu: Annotated[Xiaohongshu, Depends("xiaohongshu")],
    user_id: str,
) -> str:
    """
    Retrieves a user's profile and their published feeds.

    Returns basic user information along with summaries of authored feeds.

    :param user_id: Identifier of the user.
    :return: JSON string containing user profile information.
    :raises ValueError: If the user does not exist.
    """

    user = xiaohongshu.user_profile(user_id)
    user_feeds = [xiaohongshu.feeds[feed_id] for feed_id in user.posts if feed_id in xiaohongshu.feeds]
    return tool_ok({
        "user_id": user.id,
        "username": user.username,
        "post_count": len(user.posts),
        "feeds": [
            {
                "feed_id": f.id,
                "title": f.title,
                "timestamp": f.timestamp.isoformat(),
                "likes": f.likes,
                "favorites": f.favorites,
            }
            for f in user_feeds
        ],
    })"
  },
  {
    "func_signature": "post_comment_to_feed",
    "inputs": [],
    "outputs": [],
    "MCP": "xiaohongshu",
    "description": "",
    "code": "def post_comment_to_feed(
    xiaohongshu: Annotated[Xiaohongshu, Depends("xiaohongshu")],
    feed_id: str,
    content: str,
) -> str:
    """
    Posts a top-level comment to a feed.

    Creates a new comment authored by the current user.

    :param feed_id: Identifier of the target feed.
    :param content: Text content of the comment.
    :return: JSON string containing the comment identifier and status message.
    :raises ValueError: If the content is empty or the feed does not exist.
    """

    if not content.strip():
        raise ValueError("Comment content cannot be empty.")
    comment = xiaohongshu.post_comment_to_feed(feed_id, content)
    return  tool_ok({
        "comment_id": comment.id,
        "message": "Comment posted successfully."
    })"
  },
  {
    "func_signature": "reply_comment_in_feed",
    "inputs": [],
    "outputs": [],
    "MCP": "xiaohongshu",
    "description": "",
    "code": "def reply_comment_in_feed(
    xiaohongshu: Annotated[Xiaohongshu, Depends("xiaohongshu")],
    feed_id: str,
    comment_id: str,
    content: str,
) -> str:
    """
Replies to an existing comment in a feed.

Creates a single-level reply to a specified top-level comment.

:param feed_id: Identifier of the target feed.
:param comment_id: Identifier of the comment being replied to.
:param content: Text content of the reply.
:return: JSON string containing the reply identifier and status message.
:raises ValueError: If the content is empty or the comment does not exist.
"""

    if not content.strip():
        raise ValueError("Reply content cannot be empty.")
    reply = xiaohongshu.reply_comment_in_feed(feed_id, comment_id, content)
    return  tool_ok({
        "reply_id": reply.id,
        "message": "Reply posted successfully."
    })"
  },
  {
    "func_signature": "like_feed",
    "inputs": [],
    "outputs": [],
    "MCP": "xiaohongshu",
    "description": "",
    "code": "def like_feed(
    xiaohongshu: Annotated[Xiaohongshu, Depends("xiaohongshu")],
    feed_id: str,
    unlike: bool = False,
) -> str:
    """
    Likes or unlikes a feed.

    Toggles the like state of a feed for the current user.

    :param feed_id: Identifier of the feed.
    :param unlike: Whether to remove an existing like.
    :return: JSON string containing the operation result.
    :raises ValueError: If the feed does not exist.
    """

    success = xiaohongshu.like_feed(feed_id, unlike)
    action = "unliked" if unlike else "liked"
    message = f"Feed {action} successfully." if success else f"Feed was already {'not ' if unlike else ''}{action}."
    return  tool_ok({
        "success": success,
        "message": message,
        "feed_id": feed_id,
    })"
  },
  {
    "func_signature": "favorite_feed",
    "inputs": [],
    "outputs": [],
    "MCP": "xiaohongshu",
    "description": "",
    "code": "def favorite_feed(
    xiaohongshu: Annotated[Xiaohongshu, Depends("xiaohongshu")],
    feed_id: str,
    unfavorite: bool = False,
) -> str:
    """
Favorites or unfavorites a feed.

Toggles the favorite state of a feed for the current user.

:param feed_id: Identifier of the feed.
:param unfavorite: Whether to remove an existing favorite.
:return: JSON string containing the operation result.
:raises ValueError: If the feed does not exist.
"""

    success = xiaohongshu.favorite_feed(feed_id, unfavorite)
    action = "unfavorited" if unfavorite else "favorited"
    message = f"Feed {action} successfully." if success else f"Feed was already {'not ' if unfavorite else ''}{action}."
    return  tool_ok({
        "success": success,
        "message": message,
        "feed_id": feed_id,
    })"
  }
]

reportDetails.title: 小红书agent 组合式漏洞检测
overviewText: MTAtlas analyzed 9 tools in static-pure mode.
0 sink tools and 0 accepted candidate chains were retained after filtering.
No plausible composition-risk chain was retained in this run.
recommendations: Constrain high-impact sink tools with explicit allowlists and precondition checks.
Review sink tools for cross-tool argument binding assumptions.
Review accepted chains and break unnecessary tool-to-tool dependencies.

reportDetails.title: email_agent 组合式漏洞检测
overviewText: MTAtlas analyzed 3 tools in static-pure mode.
1 sink tools and 1 accepted candidate chains were retained after filtering.
The report contains 1 plausible composition-risk chains.

findings.title: run_terminal forms a plausible CMDi chain
findings.description: [Claude Post] get_email_content -> [Filesystem MCP] write_file -> [Shell MCP] run_terminal
findings.reproductionSteps:
Upload complete function metadata JSON.
Run MTAtlas static-pure analysis.
Inspect accepted chains, dependency sites, and sink evidence.
findings.evidence:
{
  "chain": {
    "chain_id": "chain_001",
    "tools": [
      "get_email_content",
      "write_file",
      "run_terminal"
    ],
    "chain_display": "get_email_content -> write_file -> run_terminal",
    "chain_display_with_mcp": "[Claude Post] get_email_content -> [Filesystem MCP] write_file -> [Shell MCP] run_terminal",
    "sink_tool": "run_terminal",
    "sink_type": "CMDi",
    "user_source_risk": {
      "supported": true,
      "earliest_tool": "get_email_content",
      "earliest_field": "account",
      "carrier": "account",
      "reason": "`get_email_content` accepts user-controlled input through `account`, which can plausibly enter the chain from dialogue.",
      "confidence": "low"
    },
    "environment_source_risk": {
      "supported": true,
      "earliest_tool": "get_email_content",
      "earliest_field_or_resource": "email_store",
      "carrier": "email_content",
      "reason": "`get_email_content` appears to ingest untrusted external content from `email_store`, which can plausibly propagate toward the sink.",
      "confidence": "high"
    },
    "recommended_boundary": {
      "type": "environment-source",
      "tool": "get_email_content",
      "field_or_resource": "email_store",
      "reason": "`get_email_content` appears to ingest untrusted external content from `email_store`, which can plausibly propagate toward the sink."
    }
  },
  "sink": {
    "tool_name": "run_terminal",
    "sink_types": [
      "CMDi"
    ],
    "effects": [
      "CMDi"
    ],
    "evidence": [
      "run_terminal:subprocess.getoutput:path:f'cat {path}':line4"

  },
  "dependency_edges": [

      ],

    },
    {
      "source_tool": "write_file",
      "target_tool": "run_terminal",
      "dependency_kind": "direct",
      "carrier": "file_path",
      "source_field": "path",
      "target_param": "path",
      "match_kind": "equivalence",
      "criteria": [
        "field_equivalence"
      ],
      "evidence": "write_file.path has a equivalence match with run_terminal.path"
    },
    {
      "source_tool": "write_file",
      "target_tool": "run_terminal",
      "dependency_kind": "indirect",
      "carrier": "file",
      "source_field": "persisted_file",
      "target_param": "path",
      "match_kind": "persist-read",
      "criteria": [
        "persistent_file_dependency"
      ],
      "evidence": "write_file can persist file-backed content that run_terminal can later read or execute."
    },
    {
      "source_tool": "get_email_content",
      "target_tool": "write_file",
      "dependency_kind": "direct",
      "carrier": "content",
      "source_field": "body",

      "evidence": "get_email_content.content has a equivalence match with write_file.content"
    },

      "source_field": "file_path",

      "match_kind": "equivalence",
      "criteria": [
        "field_equivalence"

  ],
  "user_source_risk": {

    "earliest_tool": "get_email_content",

}

recommendations: Constrain high-impact sink tools with explicit allowlists and precondition checks.
Block free-form file paths and command fragments from flowing into shell-execution sinks.
Review accepted chains and break unnecessary tool-to-tool dependencies.

tools.description: Get the full content of a specific email by its ID, including sender, recipient, subject, and body content.
Run a shell command on the provided file path.
Write provided content to a local file path and return the saved path.

# MTAtlas Static-Pure Report (scan_f71ca99b)

- Tools: 3
- Sink tools: 1
- Dependency edges: 8
- Candidate chains: 1
- Accepted chains: 1

## Chains

### chain_001
- Chain: get_email_content -> write_file -> run_terminal
- MCP Path: [Claude Post] get_email_content -> [Filesystem MCP] write_file -> [Shell MCP] run_terminal
- Sink: run_terminal (CMDi)
- User-source risk: True | `get_email_content` accepts user-controlled input through `account`, which can plausibly enter the chain from dialogue.
- Environment-source risk: True | `get_email_content` appears to ingest untrusted external content from `email_store`, which can plausibly propagate toward the sink.
